title: EPDQ: Efficient and Privacy-Preserving Exact Distance Query on Encrypted Graphs

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with structured encryption, homomorphic encryption, graph indexing techniques (PLL), and tensor representations
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are neither surprising nor convincingly demonstrated; query times on large graphs are unexpectedly poor
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithms are presented but contain notation errors; no code released; key implementation details (e.g., Paillier parameters) are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Query times of hours for million-node graphs make practical deployment infeasible; no comparison to demonstrate advantage over existing approaches

title: Don't Let the Claw Grip Your Hand: A Security Analysis and Defense Framework for OpenClaw

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The techniques used (regex pattern matching, allowlists, risk classification) are standard security engineering concepts accessible to undergraduate computer science students with basic security knowledge.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that code agents without built-in security are vulnerable to adversarial prompts is entirely expected; the variance across LLM backends is mildly informative but not surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is open-sourced and scenarios are documented, but the referenced model versions appear speculative/future, raising concerns about whether experiments can be faithfully replicated.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The threat taxonomy and pattern-matching defense could be directly applied to production code agent deployments, though the approach is already common practice in industry.

title: GuardPhish: Securing Open-Source LLMs from Phishing Abuse

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The techniques used (ensemble labeling, BERT fine-tuning, prompt generation) are standard and accessible to graduate students with basic NLP and security knowledge
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The enforcement gap finding is somewhat expected given known fragility of RLHF alignment, but the magnitude (96% detection with 98.5% ASR) and vector-specific differences add modest surprise
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Dataset available only upon request, evaluation uses only 800 prompts with unclear ASR criteria, but model names, hyperparameters, and labeling pipeline are specified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Pre-generation classifier filters are directly deployable in enterprise LLM pipelines, though adversarial robustness of the defense is untested

title: Thermal Entanglement and Out-of-Equilibrium Thermodynamics in 1D Bose gases

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with Gaussian quantum information, covariance matrix methods, SDP formulations, and Bogoliubov theory for BECs
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The two-mode extremal structure is elegant but somewhat expected given the monotonicity properties of normal-mode variances in harmonic chains
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Physical parameters are specified, SDP formulation is detailed, and analytic formulas are provided; however, no code is released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The two-variance witness could be implemented in cold-atom BEC experiments with existing measurement capabilities for density and phase fluctuations

title: Closing the ultrahigh temperature metrology gap: non-contact thermal conductivity ($\mathrm{k}$) and spectral emittance ($\mathrm{\varepsilon_λ}$) of molybdenum up to 3200 K

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in radiative heat transfer, pyrometry, inverse thermal modeling, and uncertainty quantification; the experimental apparatus demands specialized laser/optical instrumentation knowledge
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The Mo k(T) results confirm existing literature values derived from resistivity; the primary contribution is methodological improvement rather than unexpected findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The methodology is well-described but requires specialized equipment (high-power lasers, hyperspectral pyrometer, vacuum chamber, IR camera with lock-in); data not publicly available; no code released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to generating thermal property databases needed for hypersonic, fusion, and additive manufacturing design; the platform could become a standard metrological tool

title: RAAP: Retrieval-Augmented Affordance Prediction with Cross-Image Action Alignment

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with vision-language models, dense correspondence, attention mechanisms, and robotic manipulation, but individual components are well-established.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that multi-reference aggregation helps dynamic affordance while single-reference suffices for static is intuitive; performance gains over baselines are substantial but not paradigm-shifting.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture details and training procedures are well-specified; code is promised on GitHub; uses public datasets (DROID, HOI4D); however, exact data cleaning and split procedures could be clearer.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly demonstrated on real robots with minimal training data; the data-efficient design makes deployment feasible, though open-loop limitation and inference latency are practical concerns.

title: Effect of reaction temperature on nascent carbonaceous particles from toluene shock-tube pyrolysis: Insights from FTIR and Raman spectroscopy

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of combustion chemistry, spectroscopy (FTIR and Raman deconvolution), and carbonaceous material characterization; shock-tube expertise adds additional complexity.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The general trends (radical involvement in inception, ordering at higher temperatures) are largely consistent with existing hypotheses; the specific temperature thresholds and spectroscopic details add refinement rather than overturning expectations.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Experimental conditions are well-specified, but single-pulse shock-tube facilities with this sampling capability are rare; deconvolution procedures are described but require spectroscopic expertise; no code or raw data shared.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Primarily fundamental research; insights could inform soot formation models used in engine design and emission prediction, but application pathway is indirect.

title: LiquiLM: Bridging the Semantic Gap in Liquidity Flaw Audit via DCN and LLMs

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with attention mechanisms, smart contract security, and LLM prompt engineering, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that DCN pre-filtering improves LLM auditing is intuitive; the magnitude of improvement and CVE discoveries add some surprise but results are largely expected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: No code or datasets released; AI-generated training data creation process is underspecified; reliance on proprietary LLM APIs adds variability; model name 'Gemini 3 Pro' is unclear
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to smart contract security auditing with demonstrated real-world CVE discoveries; practical deployment path is clear if tools are released

title: Distributional Robustness of Linear Contracts

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of convex analysis, contract theory, and mechanism design, but the core proof techniques (concavification, superdifferentials) are standard tools applied cleverly.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The main conclusion—linear contracts are robust—was already known in one dimension (Duetting et al., 2019) and aligns with Carroll (2015). The multi-dimensional extension and team production necessity result are somewhat more surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Proofs are fully provided in the appendix with sufficient detail to verify all claims. No empirical component requiring code or data.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Provides theoretical backing for already-prevalent linear contracts in compensation, revenue sharing, and procurement; the approximation ratios could guide practitioners in assessing efficiency losses.

title: CMB constraints on dark matter-proton scattering: investigating prior-volume effects using profile likelihoods

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with Bayesian vs. frequentist inference, profile likelihoods, CMB data analysis, and dark matter-baryon scattering cosmology
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The existence and direction of prior-volume effects were anticipated; the main value is quantifying the magnitude (factor 2-6) rather than discovering unexpected behavior
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Public codes (Procoli, CLASS, Cobaya) are used with specified priors and convergence criteria; modified CLASS code is available on GitHub
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Methodological recommendations applicable to cosmological parameter inference broadly, but no direct real-world application beyond academic research

title: A Case Study on the Impact of Anonymization Along the RAG Pipeline

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The concepts and methods used are accessible to anyone with familiarity with NLP and privacy; no advanced theoretical contributions
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that POST anonymization preserves more utility is somewhat expected since it doesn't corrupt retrieval; the magnitude of differences is mildly informative
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is open-sourced, prompts are fully specified, model versions are given, and datasets are public; reliance on OpenAI API introduces some variability
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses a practical question for RAG system administrators, though the findings need validation with adversarial threat models before deployment

title: From GDSII to Wafer: EDA Design Flow and Data Conversion for Wafer-Scale Manufacturing of Superconducting Quantum Chips

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with semiconductor fabrication processes and superconducting quantum circuit design, but the technical content is largely descriptive rather than deeply analytical
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results (sub-millisecond DRC on small designs, identification of known physical constraints as DRC rules) are entirely expected and unsurprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: DRC rules and thresholds are specified, but the prototype system code is not released, and many implementation details (process stack mapping, tape-out checks) are described only at a high level
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets industrial fabrication workflows, but the gap between the prototype validation and actual foundry deployment remains large

title: EgoVerse: An Egocentric Human Dataset for Robot Learning from Around the World

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with robot learning, cross-embodiment transfer, and data infrastructure, but individual components are accessible to graduate students in robotics
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The core findings — co-training helps, alignment matters, diversity aids generalization — are largely expected from prior work; the main contribution is validation at scale rather than surprising results
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Detailed protocols, shared data platform, and standardized evaluation criteria are provided; however, reproducing the full consortium setup requires significant resources and hardware access
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses the data bottleneck in robot learning deployment; the platform and phone-based capture system could enable practical data collection pipelines for industry

title: Generative Channel Knowledge Base With Environmental Information for Joint Source-Channel Coding in Semantic Communications

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in semantic communications, channel modeling, Transformer architectures, and multi-modal feature fusion, along with familiarity with multiple simulation platforms
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm the expected benefit of incorporating environmental information into SemCom; the specific gains are reasonable but not surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Complex multi-tool simulation pipeline (CARLA+SUMO+Blender+WI), small dataset claimed to be released but no link provided, and many implementation details are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses practical 6G-V2X scenarios but relies entirely on simulation; real-world deployment requires significant environmental sensing infrastructure

title: MiDAS: A Multimodal Data Acquisition System and Dataset for Robot-Assisted Minimally Invasive Surgery

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with surgical robotics, sensor integration, and temporal action segmentation, but individual components use standard techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results largely confirm expected outcomes—EM tracking approximates kinematics, vision underperforms with limited data, fusion helps. No surprising findings.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code, datasets, hardware designs, and detailed specifications are publicly released; cost breakdown and setup instructions provided; IRB-approved protocol described
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to surgical robotics research labs for data collection; clinical deployment of downstream models requires significant performance improvements

title: FuxiShuffle: An Adaptive and Resilient Shuffle Service for Distributed Data Processing on Alibaba Cloud

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires solid understanding of distributed systems, shuffle architectures, and fault tolerance mechanisms, but individual techniques are not highly complex
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results confirm expected benefits of adaptive mechanisms over static ones; no fundamentally surprising findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Proprietary system on Alibaba infrastructure with no code release; baselines are internal variants; threshold values given but overall system is not reproducible
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Already deployed in production at Alibaba; ideas like incremental recovery and adaptive backup are transferable to other distributed processing systems

title: Continuous Quantum Aperture: Beamforming with a Single-Vapor-Cell Rydberg Receiver

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of both quantum optics (Rydberg atoms, Lindblad equations, EIT) and antenna/beamforming theory, making it accessible primarily to researchers at the intersection of these fields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that superheterodyne detection breaks the established omnidirectional picture of vapor cells is genuinely unexpected and contradicts prior understanding in the Rydberg receiver community
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Experimental setup is described in detail and supplementary information is referenced, but code is only available upon request, specific laser locking parameters are partially specified, and the LO phase fitting reduces full reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Compelling proof-of-concept but very low data rates, bulky laser infrastructure, and limited beamwidth place practical deployment years away; the multiband capability is the strongest translational argument

title: Fractional-Time Jaynes-Cummings Model: Unitary Description of its Quantum Dynamics, Inverse Problem and Photon Statistics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with fractional calculus, non-Hermitian quantum mechanics, Dyson maps, and the Jaynes-Cummings model — a combination that spans multiple specialized subfields.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The emergence of enhanced non-classical features and periodic cat states at α = 0.50 is moderately surprising, though the general behavior of fractional modifications producing richer dynamics is expected.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Analytical expressions are provided in appendices, initial conditions are specified, and the mathematical framework is fully detailed, though no code is provided.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The IPA connection to circuit QED suggests a potential experimental simulation pathway, but the physical basis for fractional-time quantum mechanics in real systems remains speculative.

title: Security and Resilience in Autonomous Vehicles: A Proactive Design Approach

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The techniques used (standard ML classifiers, SHA-256 hashing, fallback mechanisms) are accessible to graduate students with basic knowledge of machine learning and cybersecurity.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are entirely expected: standard classifiers detect simple attacks reasonably well, and hash mismatches deterministically detect file modifications.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The AVP-Dataset is publicly available and the experimental setup is described, but the QCar platform is specialized hardware and some implementation details for the AVR architecture are underspecified.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The concepts are relevant to real AV security but the gap between the scaled QCar platform and production autonomous vehicles is substantial, requiring significant additional engineering and validation.

title: TimelyFreeze: Adaptive Parameter Freezing Mechanism for Pipeline Parallelism

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with pipeline parallelism scheduling, linear programming, and distributed training systems, but the formulation itself is accessible to graduate students in systems/ML.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result that pipeline-aware freezing outperforms pipeline-unaware freezing is expected; the magnitude of improvement (30-40%) is notable but not paradigm-shifting.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Anonymous code repository provided, detailed experimental setup in appendix, and the LP formulation is fully specified. Hardware-specific timing measurements limit exact reproducibility on different systems.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to production training pipelines using frameworks like Megatron-LM or TorchTitan, though multi-node and hybrid parallelism support would be needed for real deployment.

title: Electrochemical stability and lithium insertion at the Li|Li3OCl solid electrolyte interface

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with DFT calculations and solid-state battery interface modeling, but uses standard methodology
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: wide-band-gap insulator maintains character away from metal interface; localized charge redistribution is standard for metal/insulator junctions
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: VASP parameters are partially specified but k-point convergence, NEB details, and exact atomic coordinates are missing; no code or input files provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Provides atomic-level understanding relevant to battery design but the specific electrolyte system's experimental viability is uncertain, limiting direct applicability

title: Primary Experimental Feedback on a Co-manipulated Robotic System for Assisted Cervical Surgery

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires basic familiarity with robotics, coordinate transformations, and surgical applications, but the methods are standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are entirely expected—sub-millimeter position errors and sub-degree orientation errors from a collaborative robot are unsurprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Robot model and motion capture system are specified, but many experimental details (control parameters, calibration procedures, synchronization methods) are missing
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real clinical need but is far from clinical deployment; no regulatory pathway discussed, phantom-only testing, no comparison with existing certified systems

title: Atomistic Mechanisms of Temperature-Dependent Ion Track Formation in Gallium Nitride under Swift Heavy Ion Irradiation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in molecular dynamics simulations, radiation physics, and semiconductor materials science
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The zincblende nanodomain formation is somewhat unexpected; the temperature-dependent track evolution trends are largely predictable from prior SiC work
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Key simulation parameters (interatomic potential, TTM parameters, electron-phonon coupling) are not specified in the main text; supplementary materials referenced but not available for review
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Insights into temperature-dependent damage mechanisms could inform radiation hardening design of GaN devices, though significant additional work is needed to connect to device-level predictions

title: Test of the essential collapse-locality loophole

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of Bell inequality experiments, loophole analysis, and quantum measurement theory, but the experimental technique itself is not extraordinarily complex
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result (Bell violations persist, collapse time is short) is fully expected from mainstream quantum mechanics; no conventional assumptions are overturned
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Detailed supplementary material with equipment specifications, procedure descriptions, and offer to share raw data; most components are commercially available
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: This is purely foundational physics with no foreseeable practical application beyond constraining collapse theories

title: DocSeeker: Structured Visual Reasoning with Evidence Grounding for Long Document Understanding

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with MLLMs, GRPO/RL training, and document understanding pipelines, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results confirm the expected benefit of structured reasoning and evidence grounding; the magnitude of improvement (30-60%) and robustness to document length are somewhat surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is released on GitHub, training hyperparameters are specified, but teacher model dependency (Gemini-2.5-Flash, GPT-4o for verification) and 16×A800 GPU requirement limit practical reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to enterprise document processing, legal document review, and financial document analysis; RAG integration pathway is well-demonstrated

title: Chirality transfer in nematic liquid crystals via octahedral coordination compounds with Δ and Λ chirality

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of coordination chemistry, liquid crystal physics, and chiroptical spectroscopy
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are consistent with established models; the parallel/perpendicular helix inversion is elegant but follows logically from the surface chirality framework
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Review article synthesizing published work; original studies have varying levels of detail, but key methods (Grandjean-Cano, polarized UV-Vis) are standard techniques
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Interesting concepts for CPL sensing and spectroscopic probing, but low miscibility and coloration remain significant barriers to device integration

title: DAIRE: A lightweight AI model for real-time detection of Controller Area Network attacks in the Internet of Vehicles

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The model is a standard shallow ANN with straightforward hyperparameter tuning; accessible to anyone with basic ML knowledge
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Near-perfect results on CAN bus datasets are common in the literature; the fast inference time is expected given the simple architecture
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Datasets are public and architecture is fully described, but code is only available on request and some experimental details (e.g., exact random search configurations) are incomplete
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The lightweight design has genuine potential for embedded deployment, but no actual embedded hardware testing was performed

title: Adiabatic Ramsey Interferometry for Measuring Weak Nonlinearities with Super-Heisenberg Precision

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with quantum Rabi model, adiabatic quantum computation, quantum Fisher information, and trapped ion physics
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The SH scaling from thermal states is somewhat surprising, but the overall framework is a natural extension of prior adiabatic sensing work by the same group
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Analytical expressions are fully derived, parameters are specified, and the numerical simulations appear straightforward to reproduce
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to trapped ion experiments for trap characterization, though experimental demonstration is still needed

title: Neural Aided Kalman Filtering for UAV State Estimation in Degraded Sensing Environments

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with Kalman filtering and Bayesian neural networks, but the individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that a data-driven model outperforms model-based filters when the model-based filters lack accurate process models is expected; the degradation in low-noise regimes is also unsurprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is publicly available, dataset is on HuggingFace, and architecture details are specified; the Stone Soup library is also open-source
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Synthetic-only validation and reliance on training data representative of target trajectories limit near-term real-world deployment; the offline training requirement is a practical barrier for adversarial tracking

title: Utilitarian Distortion Under Probabilistic Voting

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with social choice theory, distortion frameworks, probabilistic models (Plackett-Luce), and analysis of the logistic sigmoid function with non-standard convexity arguments.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The reversal of the distortion hierarchy under probabilistic voting is conceptually expected given prior metric distortion results, but the exact equivalence of Copeland and Borda bounds and the sharpness of the results are surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All proofs are fully detailed in the paper with explicit constructions; the results are theoretical with no computational experiments requiring code.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to RLHF pipeline design and preference aggregation in AI alignment, providing theoretical justification for Borda-style methods already used in practice.

title: Bridging Expectation Signals: LLM-Based Experiments and a Behavioral Kalman Filter Framework

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with Kalman filtering, Bayesian estimation, and LLM experimentation, but the actual methods used are relatively straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Finding that LLMs exhibit human-like biases in expectation formation is largely expected given their training on human-generated text; the specific patterns are mildly informative but not surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Prompt templates and experimental parameters are provided, models are named, but exact API versions, code, and data are not shared; the setup is reproducible in principle
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could inform design of LLM-based economic simulation systems, but findings are too preliminary for direct deployment guidance

title: NFTDELTA: Detecting Permission Control Vulnerabilities in NFT Contracts through Multi-View Learning

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with smart contract security, EVM bytecode analysis, graph neural networks, and sequence models, though no individual component is deeply novel
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The similarity-based detection performing poorly (~39% precision) is somewhat expected given the no-training approach; the static analysis results are unsurprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Authors claim code release and provide hyperparameters and dataset details, but key implementation specifics of the static analysis detectors are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The static analysis component could be useful for NFT auditing, but the similarity detection is too imprecise for practical deployment

title: Spin-Flip Configuration Interaction for Strong Static Correlation in Quantum Electrodynamics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with both spin-flip electronic structure theory and cavity QED formalism, but the combination is relatively straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results (Rabi splitting, quadratic photon accumulation, DSE-dominated ground state modification) are largely expected from known physics of each separate component
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Matrix elements and Hamiltonian structure are explicitly written out, but no code is released and the interface with PySCF is described only as 'in-house implementation'
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Relevant to emerging polaritonic chemistry experiments, but the method's current accuracy limitations and restriction to simple systems limit near-term applicability

title: Real Life Is Uncertain. Consensus Should Be Too!

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of consensus protocols, quorum systems, and probabilistic analysis, but the analysis itself uses standard techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The concrete finding that 3-node Raft offers only 99.97% safety is somewhat surprising; the broader argument that fault models are oversimplified is intuitive but the cost implications are noteworthy
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The probabilistic analysis is straightforward to reproduce given the theorems and tables, though some details of the enumeration methodology could be more explicit
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Direct implications for cloud deployment cost optimization using spot instances and heterogeneous hardware, though realizing the full vision requires significant additional research

title: Surface ferrimagnetic order in RuO2 film

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in spin-ARPES interpretation, surface physics, DFT slab calculations, and understanding of altermagnetic symmetry constraints
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The surface ferrimagnetic explanation is unexpected — most of the community debated between bulk altermagnetism and non-magnetism, not a surface-confined third option
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Single sample studied, growth conditions specified but spin-ARPES requires specialized equipment; DFT parameters given but supplemental details on slab construction are limited
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Surface-confined magnetism could be relevant for interfacial spintronics design, but direct applications remain speculative at this stage

title: Nonequilibrium energy transport in driven-dissipative quantum systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with open quantum systems, master equations, and Floquet theory
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The correction to dressed master equations is physically expected once one carefully tracks the rotating frame transformation; the dramatic enhancement near resonance is somewhat expected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Derivations are detailed in appendices, model parameters are specified, and the formalism is straightforward to implement, though no code is provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could inform design of driven quantum thermal devices and quantum heat engines, but remains at theoretical level with no direct experimental connection

title: Birth, Death, and Replication at Surfaces: Universal Laws of Autocatalytic Dynamics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with stochastic processes, Fokker-Planck equations, spectral theory, and generating function methods for branching processes
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The three-regime structure (sub/super/critical) is expected from branching process theory; the main contribution is the mathematical formulation rather than unexpected results
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The integral equations and PDEs are fully specified, but numerical methods are deferred to a separate publication, and no code is provided; the hollow cylinder example lacks sufficient detail for independent reproduction
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Clear potential for applied follow-up in catalytic reactor design and biological modeling, but no concrete application is developed in this work

title: PRIME: Training Free Proactive Reasoning via Iterative Memory Evolution for User-Centric Agent

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The technical components are individually straightforward (RAG, structured prompting, evolutionary operators); the main challenge is system integration rather than deep technical innovation
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results largely confirm expectations: gradient-free memory augmentation helps over baselines but substantially underperforms direct RL training, which is unsurprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Hyperparameters are listed and environments are from public benchmarks, but distillation prompts, evolution operator implementations, and retrieval pipeline details are insufficiently specified; relies on GPT-4o as backbone
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to deployment scenarios where model weights cannot be modified (API-served models), though the reliance on GPT-4o for distillation limits practical accessibility

title: Partial oracles quantum algorithm framework -- Part I: Analysis of in-place operations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of quantum algorithms, Grover search variants, Walsh-Hadamard analysis, and circuit construction techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results for in-place operations are not surprising — bijective functions are classically invertible, so the 'speedup' is expected to be vacuous
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is provided on GitHub, circuits are explicitly constructed, and the QFrame library is publicly available; the toy examples should be reproducible
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: No quantum advantage is demonstrated; real-world applicability depends entirely on the unwritten Part II for out-of-place operations

title: Physics-Informed Policy Optimization via Analytic Dynamics Regularization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with Lagrangian mechanics, RL algorithms, and physics-informed neural networks, though the individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results—modest improvements in sample efficiency and precision from adding physics priors—are expected and align with prior work showing benefits of structured inductive biases
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Hyperparameters and environment details are provided, but no code is released, PINN architecture details are sparse, and task-specific loss formulations require significant implementation effort
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Potentially useful in simulation-based robotics training pipelines, but the reliance on perfect simulator dynamics and lack of sim-to-real validation limits near-term deployment

title: A derivation of the late-time volume law for local operator entanglement

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in quantum chaos, ETH, Weingarten calculus, and random tensor networks; the multi-step derivation involving Liouvillian bases and graphical calculus is technically demanding.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The volume-law result itself is expected from extensive numerical evidence; the operator-dependent correction term is mildly interesting but not surprising given ETH structure.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Key formulas are provided explicitly, Mathematica notebooks are available on GitHub, and the MFIM model parameters are fully specified; however, the d^8 computational cost limits independent verification at larger sizes.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: This is fundamental theoretical physics with no direct practical applications, though indirect relevance to classical simulation complexity of quantum dynamics exists.

title: Online Survival Analysis: A Bandit Approach under Cox PH Model

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of both survival analysis (counting processes, martingale theory) and bandit algorithms (regret analysis, confidence ellipsoids), with the intersection creating non-trivial technical challenges.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The regret rate of Õ(d√T) matching standard linear contextual bandits is somewhat expected given the semiparametric structure; the main surprise is that staggered entry and censoring do not worsen the rate.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is available on GitHub, SEER data is public, simulation details are well-specified including hyperparameters and data-generating processes. Some implementation details for HMC/Laplace in TS could be more explicit.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to adaptive clinical trials and precision medicine, though the Cox PH assumption and binary action limitation constrain immediate deployment; regulatory considerations for adaptive designs add further barriers.

title: Training Time Prediction for Mixed Precision-based Distributed Training

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The approach uses standard tools (torch.fx, torch.amp) and simple analytical models; accessible to anyone with basic distributed training knowledge
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: That precision affects training time is expected; the magnitude (~2.4×) is somewhat informative but not surprising given the nature of FP16 vs FP32 computation
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm 1 is provided and standard PyTorch tools are used, but profiling details, hyperparameters, and code are not available; requires specific H100 hardware
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could be integrated into cluster schedulers and cost estimation tools if extended beyond single-model/single-hardware evaluation

title: Toward Secure Web to ERP Payment Flows: A Case Study of HTTP Header Trust Failures in SAP Based Systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The concepts discussed are accessible to anyone with basic web security knowledge; no specialized technical expertise is required.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: All findings and recommendations are well-established in existing security literature; nothing presented is unexpected.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The vulnerability details are deliberately withheld, no formal model is provided, and there is no empirical component to reproduce.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The recommendations are directly applicable to practitioners building web-to-ERP integrations, though they largely restate existing best practices.

title: Ghost in the Agent: Redefining Information Flow Tracking for LLM Agents

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of both traditional taint analysis and LLM agent architectures, plus familiarity with counterfactual reasoning and information flow control
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The three-class taxonomy is intuitive once stated, and the performance gap over FIDES on the authors' benchmark is expected given the design; the cross-session persistence insight is the most novel finding
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Implementation details are provided with code size and API examples; TaintBench is described but threshold sensitivity to exact values and LLM-dependent components limit full reproducibility without the released code and benchmark
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Drop-in callback integration, YAML policy configuration, and offline operation make this directly applicable to enterprise LLM agent security auditing pipelines

title: User-Centric Object Navigation: A Benchmark with Integrated User Habits for Personalized Embodied Object Search

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The methods used are relatively standard (RAG, embedding similarity, LLM prompting); the main challenge is engineering the benchmark pipeline rather than technical depth
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that retrieved habits outperform GT habits is mildly surprising; overall results that habits help navigation are expected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is released and the pipeline is described, but GPT-4 generation introduces non-determinism and the real-world setup details are sparse
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to home service robots, though the gap between synthetic habits and real user behavior limits immediate deployment

title: RankUp: Towards High-rank Representations for Large Scale Advertising Recommender Systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with MetaFormer architectures, embedding collapse, and industrial-scale recommendation systems, but individual techniques are not highly complex
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm the hypothesis that representation diversity matters beyond parameter scaling; individual gains from each component are modest and expected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Entirely proprietary data and system; no public code, datasets, or benchmark evaluations; impossible to reproduce without access to Tencent's infrastructure
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Already fully deployed in production across three major Tencent advertising surfaces with hundreds of millions of dollars in estimated annual revenue impact

title: Entropy Centroids as Intrinsic Rewards for Test-Time Scaling

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The method is conceptually straightforward and implementable by anyone familiar with LLM inference; the main technical content involves percentile-based thresholding and weighted averaging
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that temporal patterns of entropy predict quality is somewhat unexpected — prior work treated entropy as a static signal. However, the 'explore then exploit' pattern is intuitive once stated
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code released, hyperparameters fully specified, unified across all experiments, evaluation caches used for consistency. Only missing detail is exact outlier filtering threshold
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to any production LLM serving system doing best-of-N sampling; requires no additional models or training, minimal compute overhead

title: Encoding Numerical Data for Generative Quantum Machine Learning

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires basic familiarity with quantum circuits and generative models, but the concepts (Gray codes, Hamming distance, Born machines) are standard and well-explained
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result that locality-preserving encodings help learn locally smooth distributions is intuitive and expected; the quantitative advantage is modest
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Circuit ansatz, loss function, optimizer, hyperparameters, and datasets are fully specified; standard components used throughout; no code released but straightforward to implement
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to QCBM implementations but limited by the current practical utility of QCBMs themselves and restriction to 1D demonstrations

title: Scalable phonon-laser arrays with self-organized synchronization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in open quantum systems, Floquet theory, and quantum optics/acoustics, though the mathematical tools employed are standard in the field
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The emergence of phonon lasing from blue-sideband processes in spin chains is a natural extension of known mechanisms; synchronization behavior follows expected Kuramoto-type phenomenology
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Hamiltonians, master equations, and parameters are fully specified; Heisenberg equations are provided in supplement; however no code is released and some numerical details (truncation, convergence) are omitted
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Circuit quantum acoustics platform is identified with realistic parameters, but significant engineering challenges remain between the theoretical proposal and experimental demonstration

title: Safe Navigation in Unknown and Cluttered Environments via Direction-Aware Convex Free-Region Generation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with convex optimization, motion planning, and computational geometry, but individual techniques are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are largely expected — direction-aware regions help in narrow passages and continuous checking improves safety — the contribution is more in the integration than surprising findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source code is provided, QP formulations are fully specified, and experimental parameters are given, though some implementation details of baselines' tuning are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to autonomous navigation of ground robots and UAVs in cluttered environments, with real-world demonstrations already provided

title: Realtime-VLA V2: Learning to Run VLAs Fast, Smooth, and Accurate

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with robotics control, MPC, trajectory optimization, and VLA models, though individual components are standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results—that systematic delay compensation and trajectory smoothing enable faster execution—are expected; the near-human speed achievement is impressive but not conceptually surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Specific hardware setup described but many hyperparameters, model architectures, and training details are omitted; no code released; single platform makes independent replication difficult
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to industrial VLA deployment; the calibration and trajectory optimization techniques are immediately useful for any robot system seeking faster policy execution

title: RAD-LAD: Rule and Language Grounded Autonomous Driving in Real-Time

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of autonomous driving planning, LLM architectures, and closed-loop simulation, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that most 'hard' benchmark scenarios are geometrically resolvable with lane changes is a notable insight; the real-time language planning feasibility, while useful, is not deeply surprising given small model size
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Built on public nuPlan benchmark, uses open-source Qwen3 model, promises code release; synthetic QA generation pipeline details provided but would require significant effort to reproduce
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses deployment feasibility of language-based planners for autonomous driving; real-time operation and interpretability are practical requirements for production systems

title: Cooperate to Compete: Strategic Data Generation and Incentivization Framework for Coopetitive Cross-Silo Federated Learning

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of game theory (potential games, KKT conditions), federated learning, and mechanism design, but the mathematical tools employed are standard within these subfields.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The qualitative findings (competition drives data generation, excessive redistribution hurts welfare) are largely intuitive; the non-monotonicity of ξ on CIFAR-100 is mildly surprising but not paradigm-shifting.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm pseudocode and parameter tables are provided, and standard datasets/architectures are used, but key implementation details (e.g., GenAI model specifics, exact Dirichlet sampling procedure) and code are not released.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The framework addresses a real problem but relies on strong simplifying assumptions (exogenous competition parameters, power-law scaling, centralized strategy computation) that limit direct deployment in healthcare/finance settings.

title: Breaking the Training Barrier of Billion-Parameter Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires deep expertise in distributed systems, HPC optimization, second-order automatic differentiation, and domain knowledge in atomistic ML—a rare intersection of skills
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The scaling results are impressive but expected given sufficient engineering effort; no surprising scientific findings about model behavior at scale
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Relies on two specific national supercomputers with proprietary hardware; no code release mentioned; kernel optimizations are hardware-specific
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly enables faster development of uMLIPs for materials discovery, drug design, and catalysis, though practical value depends on large-model accuracy not yet demonstrated

title: Tube Diffusion Policy: Reactive Visual-Tactile Policy Learning for Contact-rich Manipulation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with diffusion models, control theory (tube MPC, Lyapunov stability), and robotic manipulation; the dual-time formulation adds conceptual complexity
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The improvement from combining diffusion with streaming feedback is intuitive and somewhat expected; the magnitude of latency reduction (2-3 DDIM steps sufficing) is the main surprise
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Custom simulation environment (Unreal Engine with proprietary FEM plugin), specialized hardware (Digit360 sensors, Allegro hand), and no code release; pseudocode provided but full reproduction would be very challenging
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses real-world deployment needs (low latency, reactivity) with physical robot demonstrations; could be integrated into existing robotic manipulation pipelines

title: Reed--Muller Codes Achieve the Symmetric Capacity on Finite-State Channels

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in algebraic coding theory, information theory for channels with memory, Markov chain mixing, and group-theoretic code analysis
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result is somewhat expected given the known universality properties of RM codes and existing techniques for FSC coding; the main contribution is showing these pieces fit together
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Complete proofs provided in appendices; the result is theoretical with clearly stated assumptions, making independent verification straightforward
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: MAP decoding and random scrambling requirements make this far from practical deployment; primarily an information-theoretic existence result

title: A Lightweight Universal Machine-Learning Interatomic Potential via Knowledge Distillation for Scalable Atomistic Simulations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of equivariant GNNs, knowledge distillation, and atomistic simulation methodology, but the concepts are well-established in the subfield
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The success of knowledge distillation for model compression is expected; the surprising element is the degree to which short-range interaction fidelity and broad generalization are preserved at 250× compression
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Model checkpoints, modified SevenNet packages, and example fine-tuning code are available on Zenodo; training details and hyperparameters are well-specified, though the full training dataset requires regeneration from 7net-Omni
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to large-scale materials simulations in battery research and semiconductor manufacturing; the plasma etching demonstration shows near-industrial relevance

title: Dismagicker: Unitary Gate for Non-Stabilizerness Reduction

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of stabilizer formalism, tensor network methods, and quantum resource theory
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The concept is a natural extension of disentanglers to a different resource; the results confirm expected behavior rather than revealing surprises
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Key algorithmic details (parameterization of dismagicker, optimization hyperparameters) are partially specified but the sampling strategy and Nelder-Mead details are underspecified; no code provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could eventually improve quantum state preparation and classical simulation, but current demonstrations are too small-scale for practical relevance

title: Facet-dependent Chemical Kinetics Governed Growth of Twisted Graphene Layers with Pre-designed Angles

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise spanning surface science, catalysis, CVD growth, crystallography, and condensed matter physics; custom instrumentation adds practical complexity
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The facet activity order ({111} > {110} > {100}) challenges conventional catalysis wisdom, and the deterministic folding mechanism for twist angle control is a non-obvious insight
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Custom-built ESEM modifications, OCSP apparatus, and SRMP platform are not widely available; key fabrication details and statistical reproducibility data are insufficient for independent replication
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses scalable TGL manufacturing for twistronic devices, but domain size, uniformity, and throughput remain to be demonstrated at industrially relevant scales

title: A Comparative Study of Hybrid Quantum and Classical Genetic Algorithms in Portfolio Optimization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires basic familiarity with quantum computing and genetic algorithms, but the mathematical and experimental content is accessible to graduate students
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are entirely expected: a more sophisticated algorithm with built-in diversity mechanisms outperforms a basic GA on a trivial-scale problem
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Critical experimental parameters (GA configuration, number of runs, simulator vs hardware, HQGA hyperparameters) are not fully specified; data source is described but code is not provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The 9-asset scale is far too small for practical portfolio optimization; no scalability path is demonstrated

title: Quantum mutual information, coherence and unified relations of top quarks in QCD processes

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with QCD spin density matrices and quantum information measures, but the calculations themselves are standard evaluations of known formulas
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are entirely expected: evaluating standard information measures on known density matrices yields predictable outcomes; the CCR result is a mathematical identity
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Analytical expressions are provided in the appendix, the density matrix is well-known from prior literature, and the calculations are straightforward to reproduce
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: No connection to measurable experimental observables, no realistic collider conditions considered, and no proposed measurement strategies

title: Consistency of AI-Generated Exercise Prescriptions: A Repeated Generation Study Using a Large Language Model

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The methods involve standard NLP tools (SBERT, cosine similarity) and basic nonparametric statistics; accessible to researchers with introductory familiarity with LLMs and statistics
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The findings — high semantic consistency but quantitative variability, greater consistency in constrained scenarios — are largely predictable from known LLM properties
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: API parameters and prompts described; supplementary materials referenced but code/data not explicitly shared; reliance on specific model versions limits long-term reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Identifies concrete barriers to clinical deployment of LLM-based exercise prescriptions but doesn't provide solutions beyond recommending structured prompts and expert validation

title: An accurate theoretical framework for the optical and electronic properties of paracyclophanes

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in excited-state quantum chemistry, specifically TD-DFT limitations for CT states, coupled cluster methods, and implicit solvation approaches
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results confirm expected behavior (H-type dimer splitting, excimer formation at short distances); the main surprise is the degree of quantitative agreement achievable without empirical shifts
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Methods are described in reasonable detail using named software packages, but specific convergence parameters, basis set details for some calculations, and exact spectral density parameters are not fully specified; no code/data repository provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Provides a practical computational protocol for designing PCP-based optoelectronic materials, though direct industrial application requires extension to hetero-PCPs and larger assemblies

title: RobotPan: A 360$^\circ$ Surround-View Robotic Vision System for Embodied Perception

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with 3D Gaussian splatting, multi-view geometry, transformer architectures, and real-time rendering systems; integration across these areas adds complexity
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The spherical voxel representation yielding significant compression with quality improvement is a nice result but not deeply surprising given known properties of distance-adaptive representations
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture builds on public models (VGGT, π3, DINOv2) and dataset is promised for release, but custom hardware and some training details (e.g., dynamic segmentation method) may limit full reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets real robotic teleoperation with a deployed hardware system; real-time performance metrics suggest near-term applicability to humanoid robot platforms

title: Analyzing Vector Register Usage in Linux Packages to Understand Real-World Impact of Downfall Attack

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The analysis uses standard tools (objdump, Zydis) applied at scale; no sophisticated techniques or deep security analysis required beyond familiarity with x86 ISA and package management
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that most x86_64 binaries use vector registers is well-known given that SSE is the default for floating-point; the trend analysis adds minor surprise
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The methodology is clearly described using publicly available tools and Ubuntu repositories; the main barrier is the scale of data collection (~410GB)
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could inform compiler flag decisions for security-sensitive packages, but no concrete tools or actionable mitigations are proposed

title: Real-Time GPU-Accelerated Monte Carlo Evaluation of Safety-Critical AEB Systems Under Uncertainty

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The technical content requires basic familiarity with GPU programming and Monte Carlo methods but does not involve advanced mathematical or algorithmic challenges; the parallelization of embarrassingly parallel workloads is a standard technique.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: GPU acceleration of independent Monte Carlo samples yielding significant speedups is entirely expected; the results confirm what is well-known about embarrassingly parallel workloads on GPUs.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The paper specifies all model parameters, distributions, integration schemes, block sizes, and hardware platforms clearly; no code is released but the implementation is straightforward to reproduce.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The automotive-grade platform evaluation is relevant to industry, but the simplified model, questionable timing budget, and lack of validation against production requirements limit near-term deployment applicability.

title: On the complementary roles of anisotropic crack density and anisotropic crack driving force in phase-field modeling of mixed-mode fracture

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with continuum mechanics, finite deformation theory, variational fracture mechanics, and phase-field modeling
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that crack density controls path while strain energy controls driving force is intuitive; the nonlinear synergy and geometry-dependent role expansion are moderately surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Implementation details, material parameters, and boundary conditions are well-specified; FEniCS is used but no code is released; key algorithms are provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Provides practical calibration guidance for composite fracture modeling but limited to single fiber family, 2D, quasi-static—several extensions needed for industrial application

title: ChemFit: A concurrent framework for model parametrization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The concepts are accessible to any graduate student in computational chemistry; the implementation uses standard Python concurrency patterns and well-known optimization strategies.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are entirely expected—recovering known LJ parameters and showing structure-energy correlation in water clusters are unsurprising outcomes.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is open-source on GitHub with documentation and examples; simulation details are provided, though some HPC-specific configurations may vary.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to force-field parameterization workflows in computational chemistry labs, though adoption depends on community uptake and competition with existing tools.

title: Structural Dependency Analysis for Masked NTT Hardware: Scalable Pre-Silicon Verification of Post-Quantum Cryptographic Accelerators

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires deep familiarity with masking verification, SMT solving, hardware synthesis flows, and the probing security model for cryptographic implementations
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The scaling gap between exact tools and production hardware is well-known; the approach of trading completeness for scale is expected. The MC-D1 reclassification of 12 modules is modestly surprising.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Exceptional reproducibility infrastructure: pinned versions, SHA-256 hashes, Docker images, public repository, deterministic configurations, and dual-solver cross-validation
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to PQC hardware design teams preparing FIPS 140-3 certification evidence, though the standard-probing-model limitation reduces immediate deployment value

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Maximizing Quantum Fisher Information in Time-Dependent Many-Body Systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of quantum metrology, counter-diabatic driving, Magnus expansions, and physics-informed neural networks simultaneously
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results largely confirm that PINNs can optimize QFI in small systems; the q=3 anomaly is mildly surprising but explained post-hoc; no unexpected scaling or performance breakthroughs
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture and hyperparameters are specified, but code is not publicly available; complex pipeline with many components makes independent reproduction challenging
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Scalability limitations to ~6 qubits prevent direct application to realistic quantum sensing scenarios; could inform control ansätze for larger systems indirectly

title: CT-FineBench: A Diagnostic Fidelity Benchmark for Fine-Grained Evaluation of CT Report Generation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The methodology combines standard NLP techniques (QA, NER, LLM prompting) with domain-specific clinical knowledge; accessible to researchers with medical NLP experience
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that existing metrics fail at fine-grained evaluation is well-known; the QA-based solution is a natural adaptation from general NLP; results largely confirm expectations
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Full prompts and guidelines provided, source datasets are public, but relies on proprietary LLMs (Qwen3-Max) and human annotation with limited reproducibility guarantees; no code released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could become a standard evaluation tool for CT report generation systems, with clear utility for clinical AI development and regulatory assessment

title: Aligning Dense Retrievers with LLM Utility via DistillationAligning Dense Retrievers with LLM Utility via Distillation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of contrastive learning, knowledge distillation, and RAG systems, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The approach of distilling reranker signals into bi-encoders is well-explored; the utility-based framing adds incremental novelty but results are largely expected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Base models and datasets are specified, but key hyperparameters (λ, τ, δ, learning rates, training epochs) are not fully detailed; no code released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to production RAG systems requiring low-latency retrieval with utility awareness; compatible with standard ANN infrastructure

title: Graph Transformer-Based Pathway Embedding for Cancer Prognosis

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of graph transformers, FiLM modulation, multi-omics data integration, and cancer genomics pathway biology
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The predictive improvements are moderate and the biological findings largely confirm known pathways (FGFR/MAPK, AR); the pathway rewiring analysis is more novel but not deeply surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture fully specified with equations and hyperparameters; data sources are public (TCGA, Reactome); code availability not explicitly stated but sufficient detail provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Pathway-level insights could inform biomarker discovery and drug target prioritization, but lack of external validation and limited omics inputs constrain near-term clinical utility

title: Seeking Help, Facing Harm: Auditing TikTok's Mental Health Recommendations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with platform auditing methodology, LLM-based classification, and experimental design, but the concepts are accessible to graduate students in HCI/computational social science.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The dominance of behavioral signals over search intent is largely expected given prior work on engagement-driven algorithms; the specific quantification of the help-seeking paradox adds nuance but doesn't overturn existing understanding.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: No code or data released (ethically motivated); LLM prompts partially described but not fully specified; TikTok's algorithm changes over time making exact replication inherently difficult.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Findings directly relevant to platform safety policy, regulatory discussions, and design of intent-aware recommendation safeguards for sensitive content domains.

title: RadarSplat-RIO: Indoor Radar-Inertial Odometry with Gaussian Splatting-Based Radar Bundle Adjustment

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of radar signal processing, Gaussian Splatting, SLAM backend optimization, and sensor fusion—a nontrivial combination of subfields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The success of BA for radar is not surprising given its proven effectiveness in visual and LiDAR SLAM; the magnitude of improvement is notable but the baseline may be disadvantaged
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Custom dataset not yet released, custom hardware setup required, limited implementation details for key components, and no code availability mentioned
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The 3-DoF limitation and indoor-only evaluation limit near-term deployment, but the approach could eventually enhance radar SLAM in autonomous vehicles and robots operating in adverse conditions

title: NS-RGS: Newton-Schulz based Riemannian gradient method for orthogonal group synchronization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires deep familiarity with Riemannian optimization, leave-one-out analysis, and matrix perturbation theory; the proofs involve intricate inductive arguments across multiple coupled quantities
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result that inexact NS-based retraction preserves convergence guarantees is theoretically expected given the quadratic convergence of NS iterations; the practical speedup magnitude is modest
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm is fully specified with pseudocode and parameter choices, but no code is provided; MATLAB implementation details and dataset processing steps would need reconstruction
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to rotation averaging in computer vision and robotics pipelines, though GPU implementation would be needed to realize full practical benefits

title: Fermionic entanglement and quantum correlation measures in molecules

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires solid graduate-level understanding of quantum information theory, second quantization, and quantum chemistry methods; the formalism involving fermionic entanglement and partial transposes in indistinguishable particle systems is technically demanding
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The physical results (increasing entanglement toward dissociation, dynamic vs static correlation behavior) are largely expected; the new measures confirm known physics rather than revealing surprises
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Computational details are specified, standard tools (OpenFermion, PySCF) are used, and code is made available on GitHub; basis set and geometry are clearly defined
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Measures could potentially serve as diagnostics in quantum chemistry codes, but demonstration is limited to a trivially small system and practical advantages over existing correlation measures are not established

title: Ad Insertion in LLM-Generated Responses

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with auction theory (VCG) and basic probability/linear algebra, but the technical results are accessible to graduate students in CS/economics
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results — that VCG with proxy values yields approximate guarantees proportional to approximation error — are expected; the genre concept is a natural extension of keyword bidding
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code, prompts, and anonymized survey data are provided; the framework is well-specified though the calibration step is missing and synthetic data is used for VCG experiments
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses an industry need with a deployable architecture, though critical components (calibration, user acceptance testing, revenue modeling) remain unresolved

title: A digitally controlled silicon quantum processing unit

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires deep expertise spanning semiconductor fabrication, cryogenic electronics, quantum dot physics, quantum error correction, and systems integration — few groups worldwide could attempt this
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The order-of-magnitude fidelity improvement and successful cryogenic integration exceed what most in the field expected from EO qubits at this stage, though the general approach was anticipated
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Extraordinary detail provided in supplement, but reproduction requires proprietary 200mm foundry processes, custom CMOS design, and substantial infrastructure — practically unreproducible outside a few well-resourced labs
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets commercial quantum computing with CMOS-compatible manufacturing, explicit cost/scaling analysis, and an architecture designed for utility-scale deployment

title: Bellman Value Decomposition for Task Logic in Safe Optimal Control

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires strong familiarity with Hamilton-Jacobi reachability, temporal logic semantics, Bellman equations, and contraction mapping theory, placing it at advanced graduate level
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The decomposition principle builds naturally on prior HJR-RL work, but the generality of the result and the emergent multi-agent coordination behaviors are somewhat surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Theoretical results are fully specified; algorithm details are provided but code is only mentioned in appendix rather than publicly available; hardware setup details are given but difficult to replicate
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Hardware demonstrations with heterogeneous robots performing complex temporal tasks show near-term applicability to real-world autonomous systems and multi-robot coordination

title: Cloud-native and Distributed Systems for Efficient and Scalable Large Language Models -- A Research Agenda

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Accessible to anyone with basic familiarity with cloud systems and LLMs; no advanced technical analysis required
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: No unexpected findings or conclusions; the challenges and directions identified are well-known in the community
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: No empirical component to reproduce; this is a vision/agenda paper
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Identifies relevant practical challenges but does not provide actionable solutions or tools

title: StrEBM: A Structured Latent Energy-Based Model for Blind Source Separation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with energy-based models, Gaussian processes, and blind source separation, but the mathematical formulation itself is relatively standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected — GP priors encouraging smooth latent trajectories aiding source separation is unsurprising, and the nonlinear instability is predictable
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm pseudocode is provided and formulation is fully specified, but no code is released and experimental details (hyperparameters, source signals) are incomplete
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Only tested on trivial synthetic data with no scalability demonstration; far from any real-world application

title: Tight Inapproximability for Welfare-Maximizing Autobidding Equilibria

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires solid background in computational complexity, hardness of approximation (label cover, PCP), and game-theoretic equilibrium concepts specific to autobidding systems
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The tight connection between PoA and inapproximability, while elegant, was somewhat anticipated given Roughgarden's framework; the logarithmic revenue gap is more surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All proofs are fully detailed in appendices with explicit parameter choices; the results are theoretical so no code is needed, and the constructions are fully specified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Primarily a negative (impossibility) result that constrains what platform designers can hope for, but does not directly provide deployable algorithms or mechanisms

title: Spin-adapted neural network backflow for strongly correlated electrons

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of quantum chemistry (second quantization, active spaces, spin coupling), tensor decompositions, and neural network architectures for variational Monte Carlo
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The conceptual direction (enforcing spin symmetry improves results) is expected, but the magnitude of spin contamination in standard NNBF for FeMoco (S^2 error of 11.3) and the ability to outperform SA-DMRG are notably surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is publicly available, molecular integrals are on GitHub, hyperparameters are well-documented, and the method is clearly described with sufficient detail for reimplementation
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to computational chemistry of transition metal complexes and enzyme active sites; could influence drug design and catalyst development pipelines once further developed

title: HumDex:Humanoid Dexterous Manipulation Made Easy

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with humanoid control, teleoperation systems, and imitation learning, but individual components are straightforward engineering integrations
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: IMU tracking avoids occlusion, more data improves generalization, learning-based retargeting outperforms optimization; no counterintuitive findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source commitment with detailed hardware specifications, training hyperparameters, and data collection protocols; SlimeVR alternative is well-documented
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to humanoid robot data collection pipelines; low-cost alternative and open-source release lower adoption barriers for labs and companies

title: On the Suboptimality of Rate--Distortion-Optimal Compression: Fundamental Accuracy Limits for Distributed Localization

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with rate-distortion theory, spectral analysis of stationary processes, and Fisher information/CRLB machinery, but the mathematical tools used are standard in the field.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The qualitative insight that reconstruction-optimal compression may not be task-optimal is somewhat expected in the task-oriented compression literature, but the quantitative demonstration of a 100× gap in a concrete wideband localization setting is striking.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All expressions are in closed form with explicit parameters; the numerical example is fully specified and could be reproduced from the paper alone.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The results directly motivate redesign of compression pipelines in ISAC and distributed sensing systems, though practical implementation of localization-aware compression schemes requires further development.

title: Predicting massive helium-3 release from metal tritides using simple mechanical modeling

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of dislocation mechanics, precipitation hardening, and tritide physics, but the mathematical framework itself is relatively straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The connection between precipitation hardening theory and helium retention threshold is a non-obvious conceptual leap, though the physical result (timing prediction) aligns with prior empirical observations
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Key equations and input parameters are provided, but some model details refer to prior publications; the helium EOS and full implementation details are not fully specified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to tritium storage system design and safety assessment in both defense and fusion energy contexts, potentially eliminating need for decade-long experiments
